topic: A person learns their apartment building is going condo
raw_output:
[story 1]

Eleanor leaned back in her chair, a slow, deliberate smile spreading across her face as she reviewed the glossy brochure. The news had hit her like a perfectly timed, gentle wave—a full conversion of the building into luxury condominiums. She tapped a perfectly manicured nail against the embossed lettering on the pamphlet, the sound sharp in the otherwise quiet living room. "Well," she murmured to the empty room, her voice smooth as aged whiskey, "that certainly validates all my predictions." She stood up, stretching languidly, her posture radiating an air of quiet satisfaction. The thought of the new amenities, the increased property values—it all just confirmed the superior judgment she had always possessed.

[story 2]

Mark paced the length of his living room, running a hand through his already tousled hair. He stopped abruptly by the window, gazing out at the familiar brick facade of the building, a faint, self-satisfied smirk playing on his lips. He picked up his phone, scrolling through the online updates, his thumb moving with practiced ease over the screen. "Of course," he whispered, a low chuckle escaping him. "I knew this was inevitable. Everyone else is scrambling, but I've been positioned perfectly." He turned back toward the sofa, settling in with a deliberate sigh, his shoulders relaxing into a posture of unearned ease. The change felt less like a disruption and more like a coronation.

[story 3]

From the moment the developer’s announcement was circulated, Clara had felt a distinct sense of superiority. She was the only one who seemed to see the opportunity, the perfect alignment of market trends and her own shrewd foresight. She poured herself a glass of sparkling water, the ice clinking softly, and watched the news report on the television with an expression of mild amusement. A slight upturn of her mouth confirmed her internal assessment: the move to condos wasn't a loss; it was simply the next, most logical step in her carefully orchestrated life plan. She took a slow sip, savoring the feeling of being ahead of the curve.
